qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Read-only dashboard users can retrieve server-side AI agent API keys
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a secret-disclosure bug in GET /parse-dashboard-config.json, while the advisory and its fix are about missing authentication and CSRF protection on POST /apps/:appId/agent.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause. The advisory is the unauthenticated AI agent route at POST /apps/:appId/agent, but the only qualifying finding is a separate authenticated config-secret disclosure in GET /parse-dashboard-config.json. A semantically aligned route finding exists in the session, but it is marked non_qualifying_vuln and cannot be selected under the qualifying-only match flow.
evidence: The advisory summary names incomplete authentication on POST /apps/:appId/agent, and the upstream fix commit f92a9ef5246d57e51696bd881a15f3b133b2bb50 adds auth and CSRF middleware around that route.
The judge input manifest lists exactly one qualifying finding: /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_f7a9358f/agent-config-secret-exposure.

That qualifying finding targets GET /parse-dashboard-config.json returning agent configuration secrets to an authenticated low-privilege user, which is a different endpoint and root cause from the advisory.
The separate /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_f7a9358f/unauthenticated-agent-route report matches the advisory-described route bug, but its status is non_qualifying_vuln.
